title: CallLint Agent Inbox Event
description: calllint.agent-inbox-event.v0 — Normalized descriptor for an incoming agent inbox event (email received, message posted, mention detected, etc.) to be inspected by action preflight. See R5 design doc.

schema_version.description: Schema version identifier. Must be exactly 'calllint.agent-inbox-event.v0'.

event_type.description: The inbox event type. Closed vocabulary for v0; extensible in future versions.

timestamp.description: Event timestamp in ISO-8601 UTC format.

account_id.description: Optional account identifier (never a secret token). Used for multi-account scenarios.

source.description: Provider identification. Never contains API keys, tokens, or credentials.

from.description: Sender identifier (email address, user ID, username). Normalized across providers.

to.description: Recipient identifiers. For email, email addresses. For messaging, user IDs.

subject.description: Message subject (email) or channel name (messaging). Never the full content.

body_length.description: Character count of message body. Never the literal content.

attachment_hashes.description: SHA-256 hashes of attachments. Required when has_attachments=true.

header_keys.description: Header keys (email) or metadata keys (messaging). Values omitted for secret detection.

mentions.description: User IDs or handles mentioned in the message (messaging platforms).

thread_id.description: Thread or conversation identifier (email Message-ID, Slack thread_ts, etc.).

normalized_content.description: Normalized content metadata. Never contains full message bodies, attachment bytes, or secrets.

action_candidate.description: Optional: If this inbox event implies a potential action (e.g. 'reply to email'), provide the corresponding calllint.action.v0 descriptor here for preflight analysis. This allows chaining inbox event → action inspection.

provenance.description: Optional source attribution: webhook ID, API request ID, timestamp.
